title: ophrys aranifera subsp. atrata
intro: Ophrys aranifera subsp. atrata, also known as the black-velvet spider orchid, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the orchid family. It is a terrestrial orchid native to the Mediterranean region and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Ophrys aranifera subsp. atrata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its health benefits may include reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and relief from respiratory issues.
